turns.content: I'm struggling with tokio::sync::Semaphore in my exercises. I thought if I just call acquire inside a function, it would automatically limit how many requests hit the API at once because the semaphore count is finite.

That intuition is close but you are missing a critical detail about ownership and scope. The acquire method on tokio::sync::Semaphore returns an Arc<Permit> which must be held until the operation completes. If you drop that permit before releasing, the semaphore count never goes back up, causing deadlocks in your concurrent API calls.
So I should be calling release explicitly after every use? But isn't tokio::sync::Semaphore supposed to handle this automatically?

You are correct to expect automation from tokio::sync::Semaphore but it only works if you hold the permit until your entire task finishes. If you drop the Arc<Permit> prematurely, the lock is never released and subsequent acquire calls will block indefinitely.

I see now. I need to keep the permit alive for the duration of my async function. But what happens if I accidentally create too many permits? Will that cause memory leaks or just slow down the application?

Creating too many permits does not cause a memory leak because Arc is designed to share ownership without duplicating data, but it will definitely exhaust your connection pool and block all other requests until they are released. You must ensure every acquire has a matching release.
Ah! I was thinking about the semaphore count going negative somehow, but actually if I just call release too many times than acquires, that causes an error instead of breaking things silently.
Exactly. If you attempt to release more permits than were acquired, tokio::sync::Semaphore will panic at runtime with a message indicating the imbalance in your concurrent API calls.
That makes sense now. So the pattern is always acquire then do work then explicitly release? And I should never drop the permit early?

Yes. The correct flow is to call acquire, perform your network request or database query while holding that Arc<Permit>, and only after that completes does it become safe to call release to increment the semaphore count back up.
